title: Panzerschreck enters service
subtitle: German anti-tank rocket launcher
fact: Panzerschreck was the popular name for the Raketenpanzerbüchse 54, an 88 mm reusable anti-tank rocket launcher developed by Nazi Germany in World War II.

title: OTs-14 Groza enters service
subtitle: Assault rifle
fact: The OTs-14-4 "Groza-4" is a Russian selective fire bullpup assault rifle chambered for the 9×39mm subsonic cartridge.

title: PTRS-41 enters service
subtitle: Anti-tank rifle
fact: The PTRS-41 is a World War II-era semi-automatic anti-tank rifle firing the 14.5×114mm cartridge.

title: NSV machine gun enters service
subtitle: Heavy machine gun
fact: The NSV Utyos, is a Soviet heavy machine gun chambered in 12.7×108mm.

title: RSM-56 Bulava enters service
subtitle: Submarine-launched ballistic missile for the Russian Navy
fact: The RSM-56 Bulava is a submarine-launched ballistic missile (SLBM) developed for the Russian Navy and deployed in 2019 on the new Borei class of ballistic missile nuclear submarines.

title: Mondragón rifle enters service
subtitle: Series of Mexican rifles
fact: The Mondragón rifle refers to one of two rifle designs developed by Mexican artillery officer General Manuel Mondragón.

title: 7.92×33Mm Kurz enters service
subtitle: Firearm cartridge
fact: The 7.92×33mm Kurz is a rimless bottlenecked intermediate rifle cartridge developed in Nazi Germany prior to and during World War II, specifically intended for development of the Sturmgewehr 44.

title: Fritz X enters service
subtitle: WWII radio guided bomb developed by Nazi Germany
fact: Fritz X was a German guided anti-ship glide bomb used during World War II.

title: Kh-59 Ovod enters service
subtitle: Air-launched land attack cruise missile
fact: The Kh-59 Ovod is a Russian air-launched cruise missile with a two-stage solid-fuel propulsion system and 200 km (120 mi) range.

title: Berthier rifle enters service
subtitle: Family of bolt-action small arms in 8mm Lebel
fact: The Berthier rifles and carbines were a family of bolt-action small arms in 8mm Lebel, used in the French Army, and French Colonial Forces, from the 1890s to the beginning of World War II (1940).

title: RPG-2 enters service
subtitle: Soviet portable rocket launcher
fact: The RPG-2 is a man-portable, shoulder-fired anti-tank weapon that was designed in the Soviet Union.

title: Borchardt C-93 enters service
subtitle: Semi-automatic pistol
fact: The Borchardt C93 is a semi-automatic pistol designed by Hugo Borchardt in 1893.

title: M42 Duster enters service
subtitle: 1950S self-propelled twin 40 mm anti-aircraft gun system
fact: The M42 40 mm self-propelled anti-aircraft gun, or Duster, is an American armored light air-defense gun built for the United States Army from 1952 until December 1960, in service until 1988.

title: PIAT enters service
subtitle: Rocket-propelled grenade
fact: The Projector, Infantry, Anti Tank (PIAT) Mk I was a British man-portable anti-tank weapon developed during the Second World War.

title: M1917 revolver enters service
subtitle: Revolver
fact: The M1917 revolvers were six-shot,.45 ACP, large frame double action revolvers adopted by the United States Military in 1917, to supplement the standard M1911 pistol during World War I.

title: 2S22 Bohdana enters service
subtitle: Type of self-propelled artillery
fact: The 2S22 Bohdana is a 155 mm 52-caliber NATO standard, wheeled self-propelled howitzer developed in Ukraine.

title: RPG-29 enters service
subtitle: Rocket-propelled grenade
fact: The RPG-29 "Vampir" is a Soviet reusable rocket-propelled grenade (RPG) launcher.

title: Comet enters service
subtitle: Medium (cruiser) tank
fact: The Comet tank or Tank, Cruiser, Comet I (A34) was a British cruiser tank that first saw use near the end of the Second World War, during the Western Allied invasion of Germany.

title: PGM Hécate II enters service
subtitle: Anti-materiel rifle
fact: The Hécate II is the standard heavy sniper rifle and anti-materiel rifle of the French Army, sometimes known as the FR-12.7.

title: Tallboy enters service
subtitle: Type of earthquake bomb
fact: Tallboy or Bomb, Medium Capacity, 12,000 lb was an earthquake bomb developed by the British aeronautical engineer Barnes Wallis and used by the Royal Air Force (RAF) during the Second World War.

title: 9M113 Konkurs enters service
subtitle: Anti-tank missile
fact: The 9M113 Konkurs is a Soviet SACLOS wire-guided anti-tank missile.

title: S-60 enters service
subtitle: 57 Mm anti-aircraft gun
fact: AZP S-60 is a Soviet towed, road-transportable, short- to medium-range, single-barrel anti-aircraft gun from the 1950s.

title: Stechkin enters service
subtitle: Submachine gun and machine pistol
fact: The Stechkin Automatic Pistol is a selective fire machine pistol manufacturered by Russian company Vytatsky Polyany Machine-Building Plant.

title: M47 Dragon enters service
subtitle: 1970S anti-tank missile of American origin
fact: The M47 Dragon, known as the FGM-77 during development, is an American shoulder-fired, man-portable anti-tank guided missile system.

title: 9K11 Malyutka enters service
subtitle: 1960S anti-tank guided missile of Soviet origin
fact: The 9M14 Malyutka is a manual command to line of sight (MCLOS) wire-guided anti-tank guided missile (ATGM) system developed in the Soviet Union.

title: 7.65×21Mm Parabellum enters service
subtitle: Cartridge
fact: The 7.65×21mm Parabellum is a rimless, bottleneck, centerfire pistol cartridge that was introduced in 1898 by German arms manufacturer Deutsche Waffen- und Munitionsfabriken (DWM) for their new Pistol Parabellum.

title: FIM-43 Redeye enters service
subtitle: Man-portable air-defense system by General Dynamics
fact: The General Dynamics FIM-43 Redeye is a man-portable surface-to-air missile system.

title: QF 6-pounder 7-cwt gun Mark 2 enters service
subtitle: 1940S towed 57 mm anti-tank gun of British origin
fact: The Ordnance quick-firing 6-pounder 7 cwt, or just 6-pounder, was a British 57 mm gun, serving during the Second World War as a primary anti-tank gun of both the British and United States Army.

title: Chassepot enters service
subtitle: French military rifle
fact: The Chassepot, officially known as Fusil modèle 1866, was a bolt-action military breechloading rifle.

title: BM-27 Uragan enters service
subtitle: Multiple rocket launcher
fact: The BM-27 Uragan is a self-propelled 220 mm multiple rocket launcher designed in the Soviet Union to deliver cluster munitions.

title: Prithvi enters service
subtitle: Long -range ballistic missile
fact: Prithvi is a tactical surface-to-surface short-range ballistic missile (SRBM) developed by the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) of India under the Integrated Guided Missile Development Program (IGMDP).

title: 12.7×108Mm enters service
subtitle: Heavy machine and anti-materiel rifle cartridge
fact: The 12.7×108mm cartridge is a 12.7 mm heavy machine gun and anti-materiel rifle cartridge used by the former Soviet Union and Warsaw Pact countries, as well as China, Iran, and North Korea.

title: PPD-40 enters service
subtitle: Submachine gun
fact: The PPD is a submachine gun originally designed in 1934 by Vasily Degtyaryov.

title: SPG-9 Kopye enters service
subtitle: 1960S recoilless rifle of Soviet origin
fact: The SPG-9 Kop'yo is a tripod-mounted man-portable, 73mm caliber recoilless gun developed by the Soviet Union.

title: Lahti L-39 enters service
subtitle: Anti-tank rifle
fact: The Lahti L-39 is a Finnish 20 mm anti-tank rifle used during the Second World War.

title: Kord machine gun enters service
subtitle: Heavy machine gun
fact: The Kord-12.7 mm heavy machine gun is a Russian design that entered service in 1998 replacing the older NSV machine gun.

title: Boys.55in Anti-Tank Rifle enters service
subtitle: British World War II anti-tank rifle
fact: The Boys anti-tank rifle is a British anti-tank rifle used during the Second World War.

title: 2S7 Pion enters service
subtitle: Soviet self-propelled artillery
fact: The 2S7 Pion ("peony") or 2S7M Malka is a Soviet self-propelled 203 mm cannon.

title: Otomat enters service
subtitle: Anti-ship missile
fact: The Otomat is an anti-ship and coastal defence missile developed by the Italian company Oto Melara jointly with Matra and now made by MBDA.

title: PM M1910 enters service
subtitle: Heavy machine gun
fact: The Pulyemyot Maksima M1910, or PM M1910, is a heavy machine gun based on the Maxim gun, that was used by the Imperial Russian Army, Navy and Air Service during World War I, then by the Red, White and Green armies during the Russian Civil War, and later by the Soviet Armed Forces during World War II.

title: Glock 17 enters service
subtitle: Semi-automatic pistol
fact: Glock is a brand of polymer-framed, short-recoil-operated, locked-breech semi-automatic pistols designed and produced by Austrian manufacturer Glock GmbH.

title: 7.5 Cm PaK 40 enters service
subtitle: 75 Mm anti-tank gun
fact: The 7.5 cm Pak 40 was a German 75 millimetre anti-tank gun of the Second World War.

title: 152Mm SpGH DANA enters service
subtitle: Self-propelled artillery
fact: The DANA is a wheeled self-propelled artillery piece.

title: BTR-50 enters service
subtitle: 1950S armored personnel carrier of Soviet origin
fact: The BTR-50 is a Soviet tracked amphibious armoured personnel carrier (APC) based on the PT-76 light tank chassis.

title: De Lisle carbine enters service
subtitle: Carbine
fact: The De Lisle carbine or De Lisle commando carbine was a British firearm used during World War II that was designed with an integrated suppressor.

title: BTR-70 enters service
subtitle: Armored personnel carrier
fact: The BTR-70 is an eight-wheeled armored personnel carrier originally developed by the Soviet Union during the late 1960s under the manufacturing code GAZ-4905.

title: Bouncing bomb enters service
subtitle: Type of conventional weapon
fact: A bouncing bomb is a bomb designed to bounce to a target across water in a calculated manner to avoid obstacles such as torpedo nets, and to allow both the bomb's speed on arrival at the target and the timing of its detonation to be predetermined, in a similar fashion to a regular naval depth charge.

title: ZPU enters service
subtitle: Anti-aircraft gun family
fact: The ZPU is a family of towed anti-aircraft guns based on the Soviet 14.5×114mm KPV heavy machine gun.

title: XM25 CDTE enters service
subtitle: Grenade launcher
fact: The XM25 Counter Defilade Target Engagement (CDTE) System, also known as the Punisher and Individual Semiautomatic Air Burst System was an airburst grenade launcher with programmable ammunition derived from the XM29 OICW.

title: ZB vz. 26 enters service
subtitle: Czechoslovak light machine gun
fact: The ZB-26 was a Czechoslovak light machine gun developed in the 1920s, which went on to enter service with several countries.

title: Type 74 enters service
subtitle: Main battle tank
fact: The Type 74 was a main battle tank (MBT) of the Japan Ground Self-Defense Force (JGSDF).

title: Steyr M1912 enters service
subtitle: Semi-automatic pistol
fact: The Steyr M1912, also known as the Steyr-Hahn, is a semi-automatic pistol that was developed in 1911 by the Austrian firm Steyr Mannlicher.

title: MAT-49 enters service
subtitle: Submachine gun
fact: The MAT-49 is a submachine gun which was developed by the French arms factory Manufacture Nationale d'Armes de Tulle (MAT) for use by the French Army.

title: 2S19 Msta enters service
subtitle: Russian type of self-propelled artillery
fact: The 2S19 Msta-S is a 152.4 mm self-propelled howitzer designed and manufactured by Uraltransmash in the Soviet Union and later in Russia, which entered service in 1989 as the successor to the 2S3 Akatsiya.

title: GP-25 enters service
subtitle: Under barrel grenade launcher
fact: The GP-25 Kostyor ("Bonfire"), GP-30 Obuvka ("Shoe") and GP-34 are a family of Russian 40 mm under-barrel muzzleloaded grenade launchers for the AK family of assault rifles.

title: Kh-29 enters service
subtitle: Air-to-surface missile
fact: The Kh-29 is a Soviet air-to-surface missile with a range of 10–30 km.

title: AGS-17 enters service
subtitle: Soviet-designed automatic grenade launcher
fact: The AGS-17 Plamya is a Soviet-designed automatic grenade launcher in service worldwide.

title: Carl Gustaf m/45 enters service
subtitle: Swedish submachine gun
fact: The Kulsprutepistol m/45, also known as the Carl Gustaf M/45 and the Swedish K SMG, is a 9×19mm Swedish submachine gun (SMG) designed by Gunnar Johansson, adopted in 1945, and manufactured at the Carl Gustafs Stads Gevärsfaktori in Eskilstuna, Sweden.

title: PTRD-41 enters service
subtitle: Anti-tank rifle
fact: The PTRD-41 (Russian: Противотанковое однозарядное ружьё образца 1941 года системы Дегтярёва, romanized: Protivotankovoye odnozaryadnoye ruzhyo obraztsa 1941 goda sistemy Degtyaryova, lit.

title: MGM-52 Lance enters service
subtitle: 1970S short-range ballistic missile of American origin
fact: The MGM-52 Lance was a mobile field artillery tactical surface-to-surface missile system used to provide both nuclear and conventional fire support to the United States Army.

title: Owen Gun enters service
subtitle: Submachine gun
fact: The Owen gun, known officially as the Owen machine carbine, was an Australian submachine gun that was designed by Evelyn Owen in 1938.

title: Gabriel enters service
subtitle: Anti-ship missile family
fact: Gabriel is a family of sea skimming anti-ship missiles manufactured by Israel Aerospace Industries (IAI).

title: Heckler & Koch P30 enters service
subtitle: Semi-automatic pistol
fact: The P30 is a polymer framed semi-automatic handgun by Heckler & Koch, available in 9×19mm Parabellum and.40 S&W.

title: 2K11 Krug enters service
subtitle: 1960S mobile surface-to-air missile system
fact: The 2K11 Krug is a Soviet and now Russian medium-range, medium-to-high altitude surface-to-air missile (SAM) system.

title: PGM-11 Redstone enters service
subtitle: Short-range ballistic missile
fact: The PGM-11 Redstone was the first large American ballistic missile.

title: SIG M400 enters service
subtitle: Family of firearms by SIG Sauer
fact: The SIGM400 is a family of firearms manufactured by SIG Sauer.

title: RUR-5 ASROC enters service
subtitle: Anti-submarine missile system
fact: The RUR-5 ASROC is an all-weather, all sea-conditions anti-submarine missile.

title: Leichter Panzerspähwagen enters service
subtitle: German scout car family
fact: The Leichter Panzerspähwagen was a series of light four-wheel drive armoured cars produced by Nazi Germany from 1935 to 1944.

title: SAR 21 enters service
subtitle: Assault rifle
fact: The SAR 21 is a bullpup assault rifle designed and manufactured in Singapore, chambered for the 5.56x45mm cartridge.

title: Schwerer Panzerspähwagen enters service
subtitle: German armored car family
fact: The Schwerer Panzerspähwagen, is a series of six- and eight-wheeled armoured cars that were used by Germany during the Second World War.

title: Zastava M76 enters service
subtitle: Sniper rifle
fact: The Zastava M76 is a military semi-automatic designated marksman rifle developed and manufactured by Zastava Arms.

title: RBS-15 enters service
subtitle: Swedish anti-ship missile
fact: The RBS 15 is a long-range fire-and-forget surface-to-surface and air-to-surface anti-ship missile.

title: Vektor R4 enters service
subtitle: Assault rifle
fact: The Vektor R4 is a South African 5.56×45mm assault rifle.

title: 9K31 Strela-1 enters service
subtitle: Vehicle-launched surface-to-air missile system
fact: The 9K31 Strela-1 is a highly mobile, short-range, low altitude infra-red guided surface-to-air missile system.

title: RIM-8 Talos enters service
subtitle: American long-range naval surface-to-air missile
fact: Bendix RIM-8 Talos was a long-range naval surface-to-air missile (SAM), among the earliest SAMs to equip United States Navy ships.

title: Daimler Dingo enters service
subtitle: British scout car family
fact: The Daimler scout car, known in service as the Daimler Dingo, is a British light, fast four-wheel drive reconnaissance vehicle also used for liaison during the Second World War.

title: SV-98 enters service
subtitle: Sniper rifle
fact: The SV-98 is a Russian bolt-action sniper rifle designed by Vladimir Stronskiy.

title: FV510 Warrior enters service
subtitle: British infantry fighting vehicle family
fact: The FV510 Warrior tracked vehicle family is a series of British armoured vehicles, originally developed to replace FV430 series armoured vehicles.

title: BMD-1 enters service
subtitle: Infantry fighting vehicle
fact: The BMD-1 is a Soviet airborne amphibious tracked infantry fighting vehicle (IFV), which was introduced in 1969 and first seen by the West in 1970.

title: BTR-40 enters service
subtitle: 1950S armored personnel carrier family of Soviet origin
fact: The BTR-40 is a Soviet open-topped, wheeled armoured personnel carrier and reconnaissance vehicle.

title: UR-100N enters service
subtitle: Russian intercontinental ballistic missile
fact: The UR-100N, also known as RS-18A, is an intercontinental ballistic missile in service with Soviet and Russian Strategic Missile Troops.

title: BT-7 enters service
subtitle: Soviet light tank
fact: The BT-7 was the last of the BT series of Soviet cavalry tanks that were produced in large numbers between 1935 and 1940.

title: Kh-58 enters service
subtitle: Air-launched anti-radiation missile
fact: The Kh-58 is a Soviet anti-radiation missile with a range of 120 km.

title: AGM-28 Hound Dog enters service
subtitle: Supersonic, jet propelled, air-launched cruise missile
fact: The North American Aviation AGM-28 Hound Dog was a supersonic, turbojet-propelled, nuclear armed, air-launched cruise missile developed in 1959 for the United States Air Force.

title: Steyr TMP enters service
subtitle: Submachine gun and machine pistol
fact: The Steyr TMP is a 9×19mm Parabellum caliber machine pistol manufactured by Steyr Mannlicher of Austria.

title: 7.5 Cm Kampfwagenkanone 40 enters service
subtitle: Tank gun family
fact: The 7.5 cm KwK 40 was a German 75 mm Second World War era vehicle-mounted gun, used as the primary armament of the German Panzer IV medium tank and the Sturmgeschütz III and Sturmgeschütz IV assault guns which were used as tank destroyers.

title: Gryazev-Shipunov GSh-6-30 enters service
subtitle: 30 Mm Gatling-type autocannon aircraft armament
fact: The Gryazev-Shipunov GSh-6-30 is a Russian 30 mm rotary cannon aircraft-mounted and naval autocannon used by Soviet and later CIS military aircraft.

title: Panzerbüchse 39 enters service
subtitle: German anti-tank rifle
fact: The Panzerbüchse 39, abbreviated PzB 39, was a German anti-tank rifle used in World War II.

title: F1 grenade enters service
subtitle: Grenade
fact: The Soviet F-1 hand grenade is an anti-personnel fragmentation defensive grenade.

title: Kh-25 enters service
subtitle: Air-to-ground missile
fact: The Kh-25/Kh-25M is a family of Soviet lightweight air-to-ground missiles with a modular range of guidance systems and a range of 10 km.

title: 90 Mm Gun M1/M2/M3 enters service
subtitle: One of a family of dual purpose guns
fact: The 90 mm gun M1/M2/M3 was an American heavy anti-aircraft and anti-tank gun, playing a role similar to the German 8.8cm Flak 18.

title: AVS-36 enters service
subtitle: Soviet automatic rifle
fact: The AVS-36 was a Soviet automatic rifle which saw service in the early years of World War II.

title: Gewehr 41 enters service
subtitle: Semi-automatic German rifle
fact: The Gewehr 41 English: Rifle 41, commonly known as the G41(W) or G41(M), denoting the manufacturer (Walther/Mauser), are two distinct and different Semi-automatic rifles manufactured and used by Germany during World War II.

title: Steyr HS.50 enters service
subtitle: Sniper rifle
fact: The Steyr HS.50 and the Steyr HS.460 are single-shot anti-materiel rifles manufactured by Steyr Mannlicher and chambered in.50 BMG and.460 Steyr, respectively.

title: Ordnance QF 2-pounder enters service
subtitle: British anti-tank and vehicle-mounted gun
fact: The Ordnance QF 2-pounder, or simply "2 pounder gun", was a 40 mm (1.575 in) British anti-tank gun and vehicle-mounted gun employed in the Second World War.

title: Vickers MBT enters service
subtitle: Main battle tank family
fact: The Vickers MBT is a series of main battle tanks (MBTs) developed as a private venture by British company Vickers-Armstrongs for export.

title: AHS Krab enters service
subtitle: Polish gun-howitzer
fact: The AHS Krab is a 155 mm NATO-compatible self-propelled tracked gun-howitzer designed in Poland by Huta Stalowa Wola (HSW), by combining a modified South Korean K9 Thunder chassis with a British BAE Systems AS-90M Braveheart turret with a 52-calibre gun produced by HSW and the Polish WB Electronics' Topaz artillery fire control system.

title: UGM-73 Poseidon enters service
subtitle: Two-stage solid-fuel nuclear-armed submarine-launched ballistic missile
fact: The UGM-73 Poseidon missile was the second US Navy nuclear-armed submarine-launched ballistic missile (SLBM) system, powered by a two-stage solid-fuel rocket.

title: Type 11 light machine gun enters service
subtitle: Light machine gun
fact: The Type 11 light machine gun is a light machine gun used by the Imperial Japanese Army in the interwar period and during World War II.

title: OT-64 SKOT enters service
subtitle: 1960S armored personnel carrier family
fact: The OT-64 SKOT is an amphibious armored personnel carrier (8×8), developed jointly by the Polish People's Republic (PRL) and Czechoslovakia (ČSSR) well into the 1960s.

title: Type 99 light machine gun enters service
subtitle: Light machine gun
fact: The Type 99 light machine gun is a light machine gun used by the Imperial Japanese Army in World War II.

title: 9K114 Shturm enters service
subtitle: Anti-tank missile system
fact: 9K114 Shturm – is a SACLOS radio guided anti-tank missile system of the Soviet Union.

title: HJ-8 enters service
subtitle: Anti-tank missile
fact: The HJ-8 or Hongjian-8 is a second generation tube-launched, optically tracked, wire-guided anti-tank missile system which was originally deployed by the People's Liberation Army in the late 1980s.

title: RM-70 enters service
subtitle: Multiple rocket launcher
fact: The RM-70 multiple rocket launcher is a Czechoslovak Army version and heavier variant of the BM-21 Grad multiple rocket launcher, providing enhanced performance over its parent area-saturation rocket artillery system that was introduced in 1971.

title: 9M120 Ataka enters service
subtitle: Anti-tank missile system
fact: The 9M120 Ataka is an anti-tank guided missile (ATGM) originating from the Soviet Union.

title: SG-43 Goryunov enters service
subtitle: Medium machine gun of Soviet origin
fact: The SG-43 Goryunov was a Soviet medium machine gun that was introduced during the Second World War.

title: Sentinel AC enters service
subtitle: Cruiser tank
fact: The AC1 Sentinel was a cruiser tank designed in Australia in World War II in response to the war in Europe, and to the threat of Japan expanding the war to the Pacific or even a feared Japanese invasion of Australia.

title: MAS-38 enters service
subtitle: Submachine gun
fact: The MAS-38 is a French submachine gun designed in the 1930s and used by French and German forces during the Second World War.

title: CIM-10 Bomarc enters service
subtitle: Long-range surface-to-air missile
fact: The Boeing CIM-10 Bomarc was a supersonic ramjet powered long-range surface-to-air missile (SAM) used during the Cold War for the air defense of North America.

title: Palianytsia enters service
subtitle: Ukrainian rocket drone
fact: Palianytsia is a Ukrainian turbojet drone missile system developed by Ukraine during the Russian invasion of Ukraine.

title: RT-2PM Topol enters service
subtitle: Intercontinental ballistic missile
fact: The RT-2PM Topol was a mobile intercontinental ballistic missile designed in the Soviet Union and in service with Russia's Strategic Missile Troops.

title: Breda 30 enters service
subtitle: Light machine gun
fact: The Fucile Mitragliatore Breda modello 30 was the standard light machine gun of the Royal Italian Army during World War II.

title: R-40 enters service
subtitle: Air-to-air missile
fact: The Bisnovat R-40 is a long-range air-to-air missile developed in the 1960s by the Soviet Union specifically for the MiG-25P interceptor, but can also be carried by the later MiG-31.

title: Nebelwerfer enters service
subtitle: Rocket artillery
fact: The Nebelwerfer was a World War II German series of weapons.

title: Zastava M93 Black Arrow enters service
subtitle: Anti-materiel rifle
fact: The M93 Black Arrow is a Serbian bolt-action 12.7×108mm anti-materiel rifle, designed and manufactured by Zastava Arms.

title: 2S4 Tyulpan enters service
subtitle: Mortar carrier
fact: The 2S4 Tyulpan is a Soviet 240 mm (9.4 in) self-propelled heavy mortar.

title: S-25 Berkut enters service
subtitle: Surface-to-air missile system
fact: The S-25, initially designated as Berkut is a surface-to-air guided missile, the first operational SAM system in the Soviet Union.

title: RSD-10 Pioneer enters service
subtitle: Intermediate range ballistic missile
fact: The RSD-10 Pioneer was an intermediate-range ballistic missile with a nuclear warhead, deployed by the Soviet Union from 1976 to 1988.

title: RT-23 Molodets enters service
subtitle: Soviet intercontinental ballistic missile
fact: The RT-23 Molodets was a cold-launched, three-stage, solid-fueled intercontinental ballistic missile developed and produced before 1991 by the Yuzhnoye Design Bureau in Dnipro, Ukraine.

title: Schwarzlose machine gun enters service
subtitle: 8 Mm medium machine gun
fact: The Maschinengewehr (Schwarzlose) M. 7, also known as the Schwarzlose MG, is a medium machine-gun, used as a standard issue firearm in the Austro-Hungarian Army throughout World War I.

title: AGM-78 Standard ARM enters service
subtitle: US anti-radiation missile
fact: The AGM-78 Standard ARM or STARM was an anti-radiation missile developed by General Dynamics, United States.

title: Type 61 enters service
subtitle: Main battle tank
fact: The Type 61 tank is a main battle tank developed and used by the Japan Ground Self-Defense Force (JGSDF), built by Mitsubishi Heavy Industries.

title: Heckler & Koch P2000 enters service
subtitle: Semi-automatic pistol
fact: The Heckler & Koch P2000 was a German semi-automatic pistol introduced late in 2001 and intended primarily for law enforcement, paramilitary, and commercial markets.

title: R-12 Dvina enters service
subtitle: Soviet intercontinental ballistic missile
fact: The R-12 Dvina was a theatre ballistic missile developed and deployed by the Soviet Union during the Cold War.

title: JL-2 enters service
subtitle: Chinese intercontinental-range submarine-launched ballistic missile
fact: The JL-2 is a Chinese second-generation intercontinental-range submarine-launched ballistic missile (SLBM) deployed on the People's Liberation Army Navy's (PLAN) Type 094 submarines.

title: Mitrailleuse enters service
subtitle: Type of volley gun
fact: A mitrailleuse is a type of volley gun with barrels of rifle calibre that can fire either all rounds at once or in rapid succession.

title: 2K6 Luna enters service
subtitle: Rocket artillery system
fact: The 2K6 Luna is a Soviet short-range artillery rocket complex.

title: RPK-74 enters service
subtitle: Light machine gun model by Kalishnikov
fact: The RPK-74 is a variant of the Soviet RPK light machine gun but is chambered for an intermediate cartridge, the 5.45×39mm round.

title: MGM-1 Matador enters service
subtitle: Surface-to-surface cruise missile
fact: The Martin MGM-1 Matador was the first operational surface-to-surface cruise missile designed and built by the United States.

title: Zastava M84 enters service
subtitle: Type of general-purpose machine gun
fact: The Zastava M84 is a general-purpose machine gun designed and manufactured by Zastava Arms.

title: 9K115-2 Metis-M enters service
subtitle: Man-portable anti-tank missile system
fact: The 9K115-2 Metis-M is a Russian portable anti-tank guided missile system.

title: Daewoo Precision Industries K1 enters service
subtitle: Carbine of the Republic of Korea Armed Forces
fact: The Daewoo Precision Industries K1 is a family of South Korean selective-fire assault rifles developed by Agency for Defense Development (ADD) and manufactured by Daewoo Precision Industries and Dasan Machineries.

title: CZ 52 enters service
subtitle: Semi-automatic pistol
fact: The CZ 52 is a semi-automatic pistol designed by two brothers, Jan and Jaroslav Kratochvíl, in the early 1950s for the Czechoslovak military.

title: Mauser HSc enters service
subtitle: German handgun used in World War II
fact: The Mauser HSc is a 7.65mm pistol introduced in Nazi Germany during World War II, and manufactured until 1977.

title: Daimler armoured car enters service
subtitle: British armoured car family
fact: The Daimler armoured car was a successful British armoured car design of the Second World War that continued in service into the 1950s.

title: T34 Calliope enters service
subtitle: T34 Calliope
fact: The Rocket Launcher T34 (Calliope) was a tank-mounted multiple rocket launcher used by the United States Army during World War II.

title: Type 26 revolver enters service
subtitle: Revolver
fact: The Type 26 or Model 26 "hammerless" revolver was the first modern revolver adopted by the Imperial Japanese Army.

title: Agni-III enters service
subtitle: Indian IRBM
fact: The Agni-III is an Indian intermediate-range ballistic missile inducted into service in 2011 as the successor of the Agni-II.

title: Kashtan CIWS enters service
subtitle: Russian close-in weapon system
fact: The Kortik close-in weapon system (CIWS) is a naval air defence gun-missile system deployed by the Russian Navy.

title: VSS Vintorez enters service
subtitle: Sniper rifle
fact: The AS Val, GRAU designation: 6P30, is the assault rifle variant that features a folding stock and was developed in 1985.

title: AS Val enters service
subtitle: Russian silenced assault riffle
fact: The AS Val, GRAU designation: 6P30, is the assault rifle variant that features a folding stock and was developed in 1985.

title: Sea Dart enters service
subtitle: British naval surface-to-air missile
fact: Sea Dart, or GWS.30 was a Royal Navy surface-to-air missile system designed in the 1960s and entering service in 1973.

title: Zastava M91 enters service
subtitle: Sniper rifle
fact: The Zastava M91 is a semi-automatic designated marksman rifle chambered in 7.62×54mmR, designed and manufactured by Zastava Arms.

title: Zastava M21 enters service
subtitle: Assault rifle
fact: The Zastava M21 is a Serbian 5.56×45mm NATO assault rifle developed and manufactured by Zastava Arms.

title: ZB-53 enters service
subtitle: Machine gun
fact: The ZB-53 was a Czechoslovak machine gun.

title: 5 Cm Pak 38 enters service
subtitle: German anti-tank gun
fact: The 5 cm Pak 38 (L/60) was a German anti-tank gun of 50 mm calibre.

title: 9M123 Khrizantema enters service
subtitle: Anti-tank missile
fact: The 9M123 Khrizantema is a Russian anti-tank guided missile (ATGM).

title: Vis 35 enters service
subtitle: Polish semi-automatic pistol
fact: The Vis is a Polish 9×19mm caliber, single-action, semi-automatic pistol.

title: MG 30 enters service
subtitle: Machine gun
fact: The Maschinengewehr 30, or MG 30 was a German-designed machine gun that saw some service with various armed forces in the 1930s.

title: M59 enters service
subtitle: 1950S armored personnel carrier of American origin
fact: The M59 was an American armored personnel carrier that entered service in the spring of 1954 replacing the M75.

title: GSh-18 enters service
subtitle: Semi-automatic pistol
fact: The GSh-18 is a 9mm semi-automatic pistol developed by the KBP Instrument Design Bureau in Tula during the 1990s.

title: OTR-23 Oka enters service
subtitle: Theater ballistic missile
fact: The OTR-23 Oka was a mobile theatre ballistic missile deployed by the Soviet Union near the end of the Cold War to replace the obsolete R-17 Elbrus.

title: Hotchkiss M1914 machine gun enters service
subtitle: Type of machine gun
fact: The Mitrailleuse Automatique Hotchkiss modèle 1914 became the standard machine gun of the French Army during the latter half of World War I.

title: MG 13 enters service
subtitle: Machine gun
fact: The MG 13 is a German light machine gun developed by converting the Dreyse Model 1918 heavy water-cooled machine gun into an air-cooled version.

title: R-23 enters service
subtitle: Air-to-air missile
fact: The Vympel R-23 is a medium-range air-to-air missile developed by Vympel in the Soviet Union for fighter aircraft.

title: Grille enters service
subtitle: Self-propelled howitzer
fact: The 15 cm sIG 33 (Sf) auf Panzerkampfwagen 38(t), also known as Grille was a series of self-propelled artillery vehicles used by Nazi Germany during World War II.

title: Sea Cat enters service
subtitle: Surface-to-air missile system
fact: Seacat was a British short-range surface-to-air missile system intended to replace the ubiquitous Bofors 40 mm gun aboard warships of all sizes.

title: Metel Anti-Ship Complex enters service
subtitle: Family of anti-submarine missiles and warheads
fact: The Metel Anti-Ship Complex is a Soviet family of anti-submarine missiles.

title: Humber armored car enters service
subtitle: 1940S British armored car family
fact: The Humber armoured car was one of the most widely produced British armoured cars of the Second World War.

title: Poignard enters service
subtitle: Type of dagger used in the past
fact: A poniard or poignard is a long, lightweight thrusting knife with a continuously tapering, acutely pointed blade, and a crossguard, historically worn by the upper class, noblemen, or members of the knighthood.

title: CZ 99 enters service
subtitle: Semi-automatic pistol
fact: The Zastava CZ99 is a semi-automatic pistol produced by Zastava Arms.

title: Barrack buster enters service
subtitle: 1990S improvised mortars by the Provisional Irish Republican Army
fact: Barrack buster is the colloquial name given to several improvised mortars, developed in the 1990s by the engineering unit of the Provisional Irish Republican Army (IRA).

title: M114 Command and Reconnaissance Carrier enters service
subtitle: 1960S tracked reconnaissance vehicle by Cadillac
fact: The M114 Command and Reconnaissance Carrier is a Vietnam War-era tracked armored fighting vehicle, used by the United States Army.

title: HS.30 enters service
subtitle: Infantry fighting vehicle model by Hispano-Suiza
fact: The Schützenpanzer Lang HS.30, officially designated as Schützenpanzer, lang, Typ 12-3 or abbreviated as SPz lg 12-3,, was a West German infantry fighting vehicle developed between 1956 and 1958.

title: Automatgevär m/42 enters service
subtitle: Semi-automatic rifle of Swedish manufacture
fact: The Automatgevär m/42 is a Swedish semi-automatic rifle which saw limited use by the Swedish Army from 1942 until the 1960s.

title: BA-64 enters service
subtitle: 1940S armored car family of Soviet origin
fact: The BA-64 was a Soviet four-wheeled armoured scout car.

title: Charioteer enters service
subtitle: 1950S British tank
fact: The Charioteer Tank, or FV4101 Tank, Medium Gun, Charioteer was a post–World War II British armoured fighting vehicle.

title: Kh-15 enters service
subtitle: Air-to-surface missile
fact: The Raduga Kh-15 or RKV-15 is a Soviet hypersonic air-launched ballistic missile carried by the Tupolev Tu-22M and other bombers.

title: 2S5 Giatsint-S enters service
subtitle: Self-propelled artillery
fact: The 2S5 Giatsint-S is a Soviet 152 mm self-propelled gun.

title: Lahti-Saloranta M/26 enters service
subtitle: Light machine gun
fact: The Lahti-Saloranta M/26 is a light machine gun which was designed by Aimo Lahti and Arvo Saloranta in 1926.

title: MP35 enters service
subtitle: Blowback submachine gun used by Nazi Germany through WWII
fact: The MP35 was a submachine gun used by the Wehrmacht, Waffen-SS and German police both before and during World War II.

title: Lahti L-35 enters service
subtitle: Semi-automatic pistol
fact: The Lahti L-35 is a semi-automatic pistol designed by Aimo Lahti that was produced between 1935 and 1952.

title: K-5 enters service
subtitle: Air-to-air missile
fact: The Kaliningrad K-5, also known as RS-1U or product ShM, was an early Soviet air-to-air missile.

title: Caracal pistol enters service
subtitle: Series of semi-automatic pistols manufactured by Caracal International L.L.C.
fact: The Caracal pistol is a series of semi-automatic pistols manufactured by Caracal International LLC a subsidiary of Tawazun Holding from the United Arab Emirates.

title: QF 6-pounder Hotchkiss enters service
subtitle: French naval/tank/coastal defence artillery
fact: The Ordnance QF Hotchkiss 6 pounder gun Mk I and Mk II or QF 6 pounder 8 cwt were a family of long-lived light 57 mm naval guns introduced in 1885 to defend against new, small and fast vessels such as torpedo boats and later submarines.

title: ERYX enters service
subtitle: Portable short-range wire-guided anti-tank missile
fact: Eryx is a French short-range portable semi-automatic command to line of sight (SACLOS) based wire-guided anti-tank missile (ATGM) manufactured by MBDA France and by MKEK under licence.

title: R-1 enters service
subtitle: Ballistic missile family
fact: The R-1 rocket was a tactical ballistic missile, the first manufactured in the Soviet Union, and closely based on the German V-2 rocket.

title: 3.7 Cm Flak 18/36/37/43 enters service
subtitle: Anti-aircraft warfare
fact: The 3.7 cm Flak 18/36/37 was a series of anti-aircraft guns produced by Nazi Germany that saw widespread service in the Second World War.

title: M75 enters service
subtitle: 1950S armored personnel carrier of American origin
fact: The M75 armored infantry vehicle is an American armored personnel carrier that was produced between December 1952 and February 1954, and saw service in the Korean War.

title: M80 Zolja enters service
subtitle: Type of anti-tank rocket-propelled grenade launcher
fact: The M80 Zolja is a portable one-shot disposable 64 mm unguided anti-tank rocket-propelled grenade, designed in the former Yugoslavia.

title: R-14 Chusovaya enters service
subtitle: 1960S theater ballistic missile of the Soviet Strategic Rocket Forces
fact: The R-14 Chusovaya was a single stage Intermediate-range ballistic missile developed by the Soviet Union during the Cold War.

title: Ikara enters service
subtitle: 1960S ship-launched anti-submarine missile of Australian origin
fact: The Ikara missile was an Australian ship-launched anti-submarine missile, named after an Australian Aboriginal word for "throwing stick".

title: Sauer 38H enters service
subtitle: Semi-automatic pistol
fact: The Sauer 38H or often just H was a small semi-automatic pistol made in Nazi Germany from 1938 until just after the end of World War II in Europe by J.

title: Martel enters service
subtitle: Anti-radiation missile
fact: The Martel is an Anglo-French missile.

title: 8"/55 Caliber gun enters service
subtitle: Naval artillery gun
fact: The 8"/55 caliber gun formed the main battery of United States Navy heavy cruisers and two early aircraft carriers.

title: Villar-Perosa aircraft submachine gun enters service
subtitle: Submachine gun
fact: The Pistola Mitragliatrice Fiat Mod. 1915, commonly nicknamed the Villar Perosa, was an Italian portable automatic weapon developed during World War I by the Officine di Villar Perosa.

title: Jam tin grenade enters service
subtitle: Australian improvised hand grenade
fact: The double cylinder, Nos. 8 and No. 9 hand grenades, also known as the "jam tins", are a type of improvised explosive device used by the British and Commonwealth forces, notably the Australian and New Zealand Army Corps (ANZAC) in World War I (1914–1918).

title: A-91 enters service
subtitle: Assault rifle
fact: The A-91 is a Russian bullpup assault rifle developed by the KBP Instrument Design Bureau during the 1990s.

title: Puma enters service
subtitle: Wheeled armored personnel carrier family
fact: The Puma is an Italian light wheeled armoured fighting vehicle family, consisting of the Puma 6×6 and the Puma 4×4.

title: M992 Field Artillery Ammunition Supply Vehicle enters service
subtitle: Military vehicle
fact: The M992 field artillery ammunition supply vehicle (FAASV) is built on the chassis of the M109 howitzer.

title: RG-42 enters service
subtitle: Soviet offensive fragmentation grenade
fact: The Soviet RG-42 was a fragmentation grenade designed by S.

title: 9M119 Svir enters service
subtitle: Gun-launched anti-tank missile
fact: The 9K120 Svir, 9K119 Refleks, 9K119M Refleks-M are laser beam riding, guided anti-tank missile systems developed in the Soviet Union.

title: R-2 enters service
subtitle: Tactical ballistic missile
fact: The R-2 was a Soviet short-range ballistic missile developed from and having twice the range as the R-1 missile.

title: DS-39 enters service
subtitle: USSR machine gun
fact: DS-39 was a Soviet medium machine gun, designed by Vasily Degtyaryov, that was used during the Second World War.

title: Zastava M77 enters service
subtitle: Type of battle rifle
fact: The Zastava M77 B1 is a battle rifle developed and manufactured by Zastava Arms in Serbia.

title: 15 Cm SK L/45 enters service
subtitle: German naval/railway/coastal defence artillery
fact: The 15 cm SK L/45 was a German naval gun used in World War I and World War II.

title: QF 95 mm howitzer enters service
subtitle: Type of Howitzer
fact: The Ordnance QF 95 mm howitzer was a British howitzer built in two versions during the Second World War.

title: Nudelman N-37 enters service
subtitle: 37 Mm autocannon designed as aircraft armament
fact: The Nudelman N-37 was a 37 mm (1.46 in) aircraft autocannon used by the Soviet Union.

title: ROKS flamethrowers enters service
subtitle: Soviet flamethrowers
fact: The ROKS-2 and ROKS-3 were man-portable flamethrowers used by the USSR in the Second World War.

title: OT-62 TOPAS enters service
subtitle: 1960S armored personnel carrier family
fact: The OT-62 TOPAS is a series of amphibious tracked armoured personnel carriers developed jointly by the Polish People's Republic and Czechoslovakia (ČSSR).

title: Breda Model 35 enters service
subtitle: 20 Mm anti-aircraft gun produced in Italy
fact: The Breda 20/65 mod.35, also simply known as 20 mm Breda or Breda Model 35, among other variations, was an Italian 20 mm (0.787 in) anti-aircraft gun produced by the Società Italiana Ernesto Breda of Brescia company during the 1930s and early 1940s.

title: R-39 Rif enters service
subtitle: Submarine-launched ballistic missile
fact: The R-39 was a submarine-launched ballistic missile (SLBM) that served with the Soviet Navy from its introduction in 1983 until 1991, after which it served with the Russian Navy until 2004.
